records.ety: [L. catechizare, Gr. <?/, equiv. to <?/ to resound, sound a thing into one's ears, impress it upon one by word of mouth; <?/ + <?/ to sound, <?/ a sound.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Catechised (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Catechising.]

defs: To instruct by asking questions, receiving answers, and offering explanations and corrections, -- esp. in regard to points of religious faith.
To question or interrogate; to examine or try by questions; -- sometimes with a view to reproof, by eliciting from a person answers which condemn his own conduct.
